question: Why is performance.now() better than Date.now() for performance measurements?

options.text: Has microsecond precision instead of milliseconds
Monotonic — not affected by system time changes

options.explanation: performance.now() is not necessarily faster. Its advantage is precision and monotonicity
performance.now() simply returns a number. For automatic logging you need PerformanceObserver

options.text: rss — total RAM used by the process

options.explanation: CPU usage is returned by a separate function process.cpuUsage(), not memoryUsage()

question: Which compression algorithm is best for static web resources?

options.text: Brotli — best compression ratio for static content

options.explanation: Gzip is faster, but brotli provides better compression. For static content, compression speed doesn't matter — it's done once
Deflate is the internal algorithm of gzip. For web, gzip or brotli are used
LZ4 is not supported in the zlib module and not used in HTTP Content-Encoding

options.text: Converts an error-first callback function into a function returning a Promise
Wraps a synchronous function in Promise.resolve()
options.explanation: promisify works with async callback functions, not synchronous ones. Wrapping sync functions isn't needed

options.text: Adds a timeout to any Promise function
options.explanation: promisify doesn't add timeouts. Use AbortSignal.timeout() or Promise.race() for that

options.text: Converts a generator to an async function
options.explanation: promisify doesn't work with generators. It's designed for callback functions of the (err, result) format

question: What indicates a memory leak in Node.js?

options.text: heapUsed constantly grows without decreasing even after GC

options.explanation: Growth during request processing is normal. A leak is when memory is NOT freed after processing completes
rss is always larger than heapTotal — it includes the stack, code, and other memory areas besides V8 heap
Non-zero external is normal. It's the memory of C++ objects (Buffer, etc.)
